name: PLaMo-100B
license: Preferred Elements dual license (separate commercial and non-commercial terms)
archDisplay: Ground-up dense causal decoder-only transformer with QK-normalization and Z-loss training stabilization
note: Ground-up 100B trained from scratch by Preferred Elements (a PFN subsidiary) on 2T EN/JA tokens; base for the later PLaMo Prime line.

results.notes: PLaMo-100B-Instruct absolute eval, 0-10 scale; GPT-4-0125-Preview scored 9.55

verificationNotes: Confirmed real release via arxiv.org/abs/2410.07563 ('PLaMo-100B: A Ground-Up Language Model Designed for Japanese Proficiency', submitted 2024-10-10, revised 2024-10-22) and huggingface.co/pfnet/plamo-100b, which confirms the dual commercial/non-commercial license and that it was trained from scratch on 2T EN/JA tokens with QK-normalization and Z-loss. Fetched the arxiv HTML directly and confirmed exact scores: Jaster 4-shot PLaMo-100B-Instruct 0.775 (=77.5) vs GPT-4-0125-Preview 0.772 (=77.2); Rakuda PLaMo-100B-Instruct 9.73 vs GPT-4-0125-Preview 9.55 — both match the record exactly. Note: the HF model card's auto-computed parameter count shows ~102B, but Preferred Networks' own tech blog and the model name consistently use the rounded '100 billion parameters' figure, which was kept per official branding. Could not independently confirm the ctxK:4 (4K context) figure from a primary source (HF config.json is gated/unauthenticated-inaccessible, and the arxiv PDF text was not machine-readable for this detail); retained from input as unable to confirm or refute.
